prevalence.trend: slowly decreasing nationally; persistent transmission in North/Northeast and some Midwest areas

vulnerablePopulations: indigenous and remote populations in endemic areas
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis (skin lesions with sensory loss, nerve involvement) with slit-skin smear in multibacillary cases; classification per Ministério da Saúde/WHO

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: contact examination and targeted community actions in endemic municipalities
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High cure rates with MDT; disability prevention depends on early diagnosis and follow-up

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS and dermatology support in endemic municipalities; referral for neuritis/reaction management
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: WHO multidrug therapy (rifampicin, dapsone, clofazimine) supplied via SUS; management of reactions with corticosteroids/thalidomide under strict control
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Late diagnosis leading to disability, stigma, and uneven specialist support for reactions and neuropathy in remote areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Established national programme with free MDT via SUS and long-standing surveillance and contact tracing in endemic areas
